text:
• Mongotune Repository Overview
Technology Stack
Mongotune is a policy engine that runs as a sidecar process alongside MongoDB servers. It dynamically monitors metrics and automatically tunes MongoDB
settings in real-time for
optimal performance.
- Language: Rust (v1.89.0)
- Async Runtime: Tokio
- MongoDB Driver: mongodo v3.4.1
- Observability: OpenTelemetry, FTDC, custom logv2 tracing
- CI/CD: Evergreen
Repository Structure
mongotune-main/
crates/
# 23 Rust crates (monorepo)
- mongotune/
# Main application entry point
- mongotune-core/
# Core framework & abstractions
— mongotune-macros/
# Procedural macros
- S policy crates
# Tuning policies
- 6 observability crates # Metrics,
docs/
tracing, FTDC
# mdBook documentation
•evergreen/
# CI/CD configuration
- scripts/
# Development scripts
run-mongotune
# Helper script for dev workflow
Key Policies
Policy
disk-write-blocking
dynamic-rate-limiting-ingress
tomalloc-release-rate
query-sentinel
execution-control
auto-restart
dynamic-connection-establishment-rate-limiting
dynamic-eviction-worker
Configuration
YAML-based configuration with sections for:
- Logging and diagnostics (FTDC, OTLP)
- MongoDB connection URI
- Per-policy thresholds
and parameters
- Supports hot-reload via SIGHUP
Development
Purpose
Blocks writes when disk usage too high
Rate limiting for connections
Memory release tuning
Query monitoring/control
Concurrency management
Automatic server restart
Connection rate limiting
Cache eviction tuning
The run-mongotune script provides commands for onboarding, fetching MongoDB binaries, running tests, and linting. Tests use mongo-orchestration for integration testing.
